mission.title: kubeflow: Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it gets renewed by cert-manager
mission.description: Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it gets renewed by cert-manager. Users encounter: "every once in a while:".

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `every once in a while:`

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
Admission webhook keeps throwing the below error every once in a while:
http: TLS handshake error from *****:34006: remote error: tls: bad certificate
Th

steps.title: Apply the fix for Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it gets rene
steps.description:
@annajung @kimwnasptd @DomFleischmann I have created this PR as a quick way to update the Kubeflow Roadmap to include KF 1.6.   My view is that we can update this in a simple way and then add more details if needed.     Please review, improve as you see needed.   Thanks.   Josh

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Admission webhook doesn't pick up new cert when it is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `every once in a while:` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: @annajung @kimwnasptd @DomFleischmann I have created this PR as a quick way to update the Kubeflow Roadmap to include KF 1.6.   My view is that we can update this in a simple way and then add more details if needed.     Please review, improve as you see needed.   Thanks.   Josh. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
